error_signature: Postgres node accessing all upstream data
title: Postgres node accessing unwanted upstream data
symptom: The Postgres node is updating fields based on data from all previous nodes, not just the intended input. This leads to incorrect updates when field names match with the original payload.
applies_when: Data is being processed from multiple upstream nodes
not_this_if: Only one upstream node is connected
Data is correctly filtered before reaching the Postgres node
root_cause: By default, n8n nodes can access all data from upstream nodes, which can lead to unintended data being processed if not properly filtered. This behavior is normal and requires explicit configuration to restrict data access.
fix_steps: In the Postgres node settings, configure it to only use input from the directly connected node.
Alternatively, add a Set node after your Code node to specify only the fields you want to pass to the Postgres node.
agent_instruction: Configure the Postgres node to restrict input to only the connected node or use a Set node to filter the data.
